Chambal Fertilisers CFO Steps Down; Successor Named
data.description:
Chambal Fertilisers and Chemicals Chief Financial Officer Anand Agarwal resigned on May 8 to pursue an external opportunity.

He will be relieved from his duties on May 12, according to a Thursday filing on the Indian bourses.

The board has appointed Anuj Jain as CFO effective May 9.

Jain has been with the company since 2001. He has over 38 years of experience across finance, accounts, internal audit, taxation, and secretarial functions.

data.title: Malaysia Faces Growth Risks, Rate Cut Likely Amid Tariff Uncertainty — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's export-heavy economy may remain vulnerable to escalating U.S.-China trade tensions, prompting MUFG to cut its 2025 GDP forecast to 4.1% from its previous estimate of 4.7%. Investors have scaled back earlier over-optimism on the ringgit, with further weakening likely amid ongoing macroeconomic challenges, it says. MUFG expects Bank Negara to cut rates by 25 bps this year--possibly in July--bringing the policy rate to 2.75%, as inflation stays benign and dovish rhetoric grows. If the full reciprocal tariff is applied after its 90-day hiatus, it could shave an additional 0.4 ppt from Malaysia's GDP growth, it warns. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Lupin Wins US FDA Approval for HIV Drug Raltegravir With 180-Day Market Exclusivity
data.description:
Lupin received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration for its generic version of Raltegravir Tablets USP, 600 mg, the bioequivalent of Isentress HD by Merck, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

As the first-to-file applicant, Lupin is eligible for 180 days of exclusive marketing rights in the US.

The drug, used in combination therapy for HIV-1 treatment, will be manufactured at the company's Nagpur facility.

data.title: Bain Capital to sell China data centre business likely valued at over $4 billion, sources say
data.description:
U.S. investment firm Bain Capital is seeking to sell the China business of data centre operator WinTriX DC Group, in a deal that could value the business at over $4 billion, two people with knowledge of the situation said.

Bain Capital has engaged with advisers who have held preliminary conversations with potential buyers in recent months, said the people, declining to be named as the information was not public.

The China business of WinTriX, formerly known as Chindata Group Holdings, has estimated 2025 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation (EBITDA) of close to 4 billion yuan, they said.

Bain Capital declined to comment. The data centre operator did not respond to a Reuters request for comment.

The potential deal comes nearly two years after Bain Capital took Nasdaq-listed Chindata private in a $3.16 billion deal.

Bain Capital first acquired Chinese data centre operator Chindata in 2019 and merged it with Southeast Asia data centre operator Bridge Data Centres in the same year. The listed entity was a combination of both.

Since the take-private, the Boston-based firm separated the two businesses again under WinTriX, said a third person with knowledge of the situation.

The sale also comes as data centre valuations have soared in the last few years, driven by rapid developments of artificial intelligence.

Last year Australian data centre operator AirTrunk was sold to a Blackstone-led consortium for over 20 times its forward core earnings, Reuters reported.

WinTriX's Chinese rival GDS Holdings is currently trading at a price-to-earnings multiple of 8.48 times, LSEG data showed.

Fitch Ratings however in February downgraded WinTriX's long-term foreign- and local-currency issuer default ratings to "BB" from "BBB" with a stable outlook, which it said reflected its expectations that WinTriX would face significantly higher business risks as it changed its strategy to focus on overseas investment.

Fitch cited slower hyperscale data centre demand and higher competition in China as one of the additional risks WinTriX would face.

WinTriX counts social media giant Bytedance as its largest customer, which contributed to 86% of its revenue in 2022, according to the Fitch report.

Outside China, it also operates data centres in India and Malaysia.

In March, WinTriX's unit Bridge Data Centres, which operates data centres outside China, secured a $2.8 billion bank financing to fuel its data centre expansion, it said at the time.

Bain Capital will keep its control of Bridge Data Centres for the time being, said the sources.

data.title: Nelnet Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Nelnet Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $2.26​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.81. The lone analyst forecast for the quarter was for earnings of $1.92 per share.

  • Revenue rose 3.4% to $398.49 million from a year ago; analysts expected $393.27 million.

  • Nelnet Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $2.26​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $82.56 million.

  • Nelnet Inc shares had fallen by 4.9% this quarter and lost 1.2% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 4% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is no "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the consumer lending peer group is also "hold"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Nelnet Inc is $115.00

data.title: Banpu Target Price Lowered to THB4.80 From THB5.00 by UOB Kay Hian >BANPU.TH
Highfield Resources CEO Steps Down
data.description:
Highfield Resources Chief Executive Officer Ignacio Salazar has resigned, effective immediately, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Salazar joined the mining company as CEO in July 2020, per the filing.

data.title: Coastal's Ethanol Unit to Start Commercial Production May 19; Odisha Plant on Hold
data.description:
Coastal said its wholly owned unit Coastal Biotech has completed trial runs for its ethanol project and will begin commercial operations on May 19, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The company expects the ethanol business to boost revenue.

Meanwhile, another subsidiary, Continental Fisheries India, has put its Odisha processing plant project on hold due to geopolitical uncertainties, including proposed US tariffs on Indian exports.

Civil work at the site had begun after land acquisition, but the unit is currently outsourcing shrimp processing to third-party facilities. Coastal said it will reassess the project once market conditions improve.

data.title: Brigade Group to Develop INR20 Billion Commercial Project in Bengaluru
data.description:
BRIGADE ENTERPRISES has acquired an 11-acre plot in Bengaluru to develop a 20 billion Indian rupees commercial project with 1.5 million sq ft of leasable space, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The company said the Grade A office project will focus on sustainability and support its net zero goals.

data.title: Lost Money on NET Power, Inc.(NPWR)? Join Class Action Suit Seeking Recovery - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against NET Power, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between June 9, 2023 and March 7, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) Net Power was unlikely to complete its first utility-scale plant, Project Permian, on schedule and the project was likely to be significantly more expensive than defendants had represented, because of, inter alia, supply chain issues and numerous site- and region-specific challenges; (ii) accordingly, defendants' projections regarding the time and capital needed to complete Project Permian were unrealistic; (iii) the increased time and capital needed to complete Project Permian were likely to have a significant negative impact on the Company's business and financial results; and (iv) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

data.title: Affirm Holdings Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Affirm Holdings Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 1 cent​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of -43 cents. The mean expectation of twenty one analysts for the quarter was for a loss of 3 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -21 cents to 14 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 35.9% to $783.14 million from a year ago; analysts expected $783.03 million.

  • Affirm Holdings Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 1 cent​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $2.8 million.

  • Affirm Holdings Inc shares had risen by 14.1% this quarter and lost 15.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 37.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

data.title: Viceroy Hotels Enters Operating Agreement with Marriott
data.description:
Viceroy Hotels has entered into an operating agreement with Marriott Hotels India Private Limited for the management of its hotel property in Hyderabad, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourse.

The hotel, to be operated under the "Courtyard by Marriott" brand, will span approximately 7,000 square yards and offer around 200 guest rooms. The agreement will last until the end of the 30th full fiscal year following the hotel's opening.

This partnership is aimed at enhancing operational standards and aligning with global hospitality practices.

Additionally, the hotel's construction site is owned by the relatives of one of Viceroy's directors, with shareholder approval granted at the company's Extraordinary General Meeting in April 2025.

data.title: Rupee likely to weaken more on widening India-Pakistan conflict
data.description:
The Indian rupee looks set to decline further on Friday, pressured by the widening conflict between India and Pakistan that may trigger increase hedging and speculative activity.

The 1-month non-deliverable forward indicated that the rupee will open at 85.80 to 85.90 to the U.S. dollar compared with 85.71 in the previous session.

The rupee on Wednesday slumped 1.04%, recording its worst session in more than two years. The currency came under pressure in the afternoon session after India reported Pakistan's attempts to engage military targets.

"Until yesterday afternoon, markets had largely priced in a view that there would be no meaningful worsening in India-Pakistan tensions," a currency trader at a Mumbai-based bank said.

"That assumption is now being reassessed, and positioning in the rupee will likely need to be adjusted accordingly," and it may lead to a pick-up in hedging and speculative activity, the trader said.

Pakistan and India accused each other of launching drone attacks on Thursday, and Islamabad’s Defense Minister said further retaliation was “increasingly certain”.

India said military stations were attacked by Pakistani drones and missiles.

Indian equities were set to open more than 1% lower. In the two sessions following the rise in tensions, the activity of foreign investors indicates that they largely held the view that the situation would not have a lasting impact on the Indian economy, analysts said.

Preliminary data showed that foreign investors were net buyers of Indian equities on Thursday, after buying approximately $350 million on Wednesday.

"It will be interesting to see how the numbers pan out in today’s session. There’s a sense that the landscape has shifted,” said Kunal Kurani, Assistant Vice President at Mecklai Financial.

Other Asian currencies largely weakened on Friday, while the U.S. dollar rose against its major counterparts, creating additional headwinds for the rupee.

KEY INDICATORS:

** One-month non-deliverable rupee (INRNDFOR=) forward at 86.12; onshore one-month forward premium at 18.75 paise

** Dollar index up at 100.76

** Brent crude futures up 0.1% at $62.9 per barrel

** Ten-year U.S. note yield at 4.37%

** As per NSDL data, foreign investors bought a net $349.1 mln worth of Indian shares on May 7

** NSDL data shows foreign investors sold a net $115.7 mln worth of Indian bonds on May 7

data.title: Dollar Set for Third Weekly Rise
data.description:
The US dollar index climbed toward 101 on Friday, poised for its third consecutive weekly gain as improving global trade sentiment and fading expectations of imminent rate cuts supported the greenback.

The rally was fueled in part by President Donald Trump’s announcement of a preliminary trade deal with the UK, the first since the US imposed sweeping tariffs last month.

Trump also suggested that additional agreements could follow and hinted at a potential easing of tariffs on China, depending on the outcome of upcoming trade talks in Switzerland.

On the monetary policy front, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell dismissed the idea of a preemptive rate cut in response to potential economic fallout from tariffs.

Powell also warned of elevated risks to both inflation and unemployment, signaling caution before adjusting policy further.

The dollar posted its strongest gains this week against the euro, New Zealand dollar, and Australian dollar.

data.title: GR Infraprojects Secures INR2.6 Billion Contract from Western Railways
data.description:
GR Infraprojects received a letter of acceptance from Western Railways for a project involving the gauge conversion of 38.9 kilometers of track and associated works, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The project, valued at 2.62 billion Indian rupees, includes earthwork, track linking, and the construction of platforms, bridges, and other infrastructure.

The completion period is set for 730 days from the appointed date.

data.title: Domain Enters Binding Scheme Implementation Deed With CoStar
data.description:
Domain Holdings Australia entered a binding scheme implementation deed with CoStar Group for the Nasdaq-listed real estate firm to acquire all the remaining shares in the company for AU$4.43 per share in cash, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

CoStar, through Andromeda Australia SubCo, currently holds 16.9% of Domain, the filing said.

The deal is subject to shareholder and court approvals, with a scheme meeting expected in mid-August, the filing added.

The Domain board recommends shareholders vote in favor of the scheme in the absence of a superior proposal and subject to an independent expert's assessment.

Nine Entertainment Holdings , the majority shareholder of Domain, intends to vote in favor of the proposed scheme, it said in a separate bourse filing.

Shares of Domain Holdings Australia rose 3% in recent Friday trade, while Nine Entertainment shares climbed past 5%.

data.title: Business Turnover in Australia Rises 0.4% in March
data.description:
Business turnover across 13 Australian industries rose 0.4% in seasonally adjusted terms in March, data from the Australian Bureau of Statistics showed Friday.

Wholesale trade led growth, with business turnover in the industry up 2.2%. This was followed by a 2% increase in arts and recreation services and a 1.8% rise in information media and telecommunications.

In contrast, the biggest decline was seen in electricity, gas, water, and waste services, with business turnover down 4.1%. Transport, postal, and warehousing followed with a 3.9% drop. Both industries experienced disruptions due to adverse weather conditions across Queensland, New South Wales, and Western Australia, according to Robert Ewing, the bureau's head of business statistics.

Compared with the year-ago period, business turnover was up 4.6% in March.

data.title: Websol Energy Bags Solar Module Supply Deal
data.description:
Websol Energy System signed a supply agreement with CRI Pumps for up to 250 MW of Mono-PERC DCR solar modules, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The modules rated at 500Wp, 520Wp, 525Wp, and 535Wp will support CRI Pumps' solar-powered pumping systems under the PM-KUSUM scheme.

The deal falls under India's Domestic Content Requirement (DCR) framework, ensuring the use of locally-made components.
